restaurants.name: Nanjing Impressions
restaurants.ambience: Immersive old-Nanjing setting with lanterns, live folk music, and authentic flavors

restaurants.name: Jiangnan Tang Restaurant
restaurants.ambience: Upscale modern Jiangsu dining with beautifully plated dishes and tranquil interiors

restaurants.name: Nanjing Da Pai Dang
restaurants.ambience: Retro-style restaurant recreating the charm of 1930s Nanjing street dining, with live traditional music

restaurants.name: The House of Roosevelt
restaurants.ambience: Elegant fine-dining restaurant combining Western sophistication with Jiangsu influences — ideal for a refined final night in Nanjing

attractions.name: Confucius Temple
attractions.description: Originally built in 1034, the Confucius Temple complex honors China's greatest philosopher. Wander among traditional halls, colorful lanterns, and stone bridges reflecting in the river below. This is the spiritual and cultural heart of Nanjing — lively yet steeped in history

attractions.name: Qinhuai River Scenic Area
attractions.description: Known as the "Mother River of Nanjing," the Qinhuai glows beautifully at dusk when lanterns light up the waterside teahouses and willow-lined banks. A short boat cruise reveals elegant pavilions and reflections of history in every ripple

attractions.name: Ming Xiaoling Mausoleum
attractions.description: Hidden among purple mountain forests, this UNESCO World Heritage Site is the resting place of Emperor Zhu Yuanzhang, the Ming dynasty's founder. The Sacred Way, lined with centuries-old stone animals and mythical creatures, leads to the emperor's tomb — serene, majestic, and steeped in history

attractions.name: Sun Yat-sen Mausoleum
attractions.description: One of China's most revered memorials, this grand mausoleum honors Dr. Sun Yat-sen, the founding father of modern China. Climb the 392 steps framed by pine trees and blue-glazed tiles to reach the main hall, where the peaceful view of Nanjing unfolds beneath you

attractions.name: Presidential Palace
attractions.description: Once the political heart of the Republic of China, the Presidential Palace blends classical gardens with Western-style halls. Explore meeting chambers, offices, and tranquil courtyards that tell the story of modern China's transformation from empire to republic

local_dishes.name: Duck Blood Vermicelli Soup
local_dishes.description: Traditional Nanjing soup with duck blood and noodles

local_dishes.name: Duck Meat Buns
local_dishes.description: Steamed buns filled with duck meat
